definition: to drive against, drive home , ἔγχος (Iliad by Homer); ἐπέρεισε δὲ ἶν᾽ ἀπέλεθρον put  vast strength to  it, (Homer):—; ἐπ. τὴν φάλαγγά τινι to bring the whole force of  the phalanx against, (Plutarch):—;Mid., λαίφη προτόνοις ἐπερειδόμεναι staying their  sails on ropes, (Euripides):—;Pass. to lean  or bear upon , τινι (Aristophanes Comicus): absolute to resist with all one's force , (Aristophanes Comicus) (ML)
